chunk_summary: This section focuses on culturally sensitive, disease-adapted meal planning for pediatric conditions, emphasizing balanced nutrition, avoiding over-restriction, and addressing specific dietary needs for obesity, undernutrition, constipation, diarrhea, food allergies, and celiac disease.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of avoiding over-restriction in pediatric meal planning to prevent growth faltering.
Learn tailored dietary strategies for managing pediatric obesity, undernutrition, and gastrointestinal conditions.
Recognize the risks of broad food elimination without proper diagnosis in food allergies and celiac disease.
Apply culturally sensitive approaches to pediatric nutrition counseling.
key_facts: Over-restriction in meal planning can lead to growth faltering and poor adherence.
For pediatric obesity, eliminate sugar-sweetened beverages first and increase protein and fiber intake at meals.
In undernutrition, increase meal frequency and energy density using thicker consistency, added oil/ghee, and nutrient-dense snacks.
Constipation management includes increasing water and fiber intake while reducing excessive milk only if it displaces solids.
For suspected celiac disease, avoid starting a gluten-free diet before diagnostic testing to preserve diagnostic yield.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Prolonged 'bland diets' delay weight regain in recurrent diarrhea or prolonged loose stools. (Source: https://tnaap.org/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/peds_2022060640.pdf)
